P3DSN-001.title: Figma's 2025 AI report: Perspectives From Designers and Developers
P3DSN-001.author: Figma
P3DSN-001.excerpt: Survey of 2,500 Figma users. 1 in 3 launching AI-powered products in 2025 (50% YoY growth). 85% of designers and developers say learning to work with AI is essential to future success. 78% agree AI 'significantly enhances the efficiency of my work,' but only 32% say they can 'rely on the output of AI in their work.' Developer satisfaction with AI tools: 82% vs designer satisfaction: 69%. Developers say AI improves quality: 68% vs designers: 54%. Developers using AI for core dev tasks (code gen): 59% vs designers using AI in core design work (asset gen): 31%. Only 20% report majority of projects are AI-powered.
P3DSN-001.relevance: Best primary source on designer-developer AI adoption gap; quantifies efficiency vs trust split.
P3DSN-001.credibility: high - large-N vendor survey but Figma has selection bias toward AI-receptive users

P3DSN-002.title: The State of AI in Design: What we learned from 400+ designers
P3DSN-002.author: Foundation Capital
P3DSN-002.excerpt: Survey of nearly 400 designers. 96% are self-taught in discovering AI tools. Startups adopt AI 'twice as fast' as large companies. Key insight: 'AI adoption is grassroots, not top-down.' Larger companies face slower adoption due to privacy, security, and legal constraints. 'Human creativity and judgment matter more than ever' as AI-generated outputs become routine.
P3DSN-002.relevance: Establishes grassroots/self-taught adoption pattern - critical for understanding why designers need showcase platforms.
P3DSN-002.credibility: high - venture fund report, biased toward AI-positive but n=400 is solid

P3DSN-003.title: AI in Design Report 2026
P3DSN-003.author: State of AI in Design
P3DSN-003.excerpt: Survey of 906 designers across 60+ countries, 25+ interviews. 'Designers are using double the number of off-the-shelf tools than they did in 2025.' Approximately half of respondents 'have pushed AI-generated code to production.' 'Roles are blurring as designers take on PM and engineering work, and vice versa.' Largest improvement area identified: 'reliable output quality.'
P3DSN-003.relevance: Latest independent survey; documents tool proliferation and role blurring (PM+eng+design hybridization).
P3DSN-003.credibility: medium-high - independent industry report, sample skews self-selected

P3DSN-004.title: Designers most likely among creatives to believe AI dulls creativity
P3DSN-004.author: Dezeen
P3DSN-004.excerpt: Survey finding (Figma 2025): '81% of designers said that they believed AI dulled creativity.' Designers expressed strongest skepticism among creative professions about AI's effect on craft. Tension between efficiency gains and creative concern.
P3DSN-004.relevance: Counter-narrative to 'designers love AI' - documents the resistance/anxiety side.
P3DSN-004.credibility: high - mainstream design press citing Figma data

P3DSN-005.title: Welcome to the entry-level void: what happens when junior design jobs disappear?
P3DSN-005.author: It's Nice That
P3DSN-005.excerpt: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei predicts AI could eliminate half of all entry-level white-collar jobs within five years. Dev Makker (Parsons grad) made 500+ cold LinkedIn connections to land first internship; estimates 'single-digit percentage' of cohort has employment. 'Retouching, cropping, copy variants, banner production – once standard junior fare – are now done with a single prompt.' RISD educator Kelin Carolyn Zhang: students accepting unpaid internships in 'desperate market.' Naheel Jawaid: 'It's really hard to get mentorship right now.' Portfolios sent into 'empty void.'
P3DSN-005.relevance: Strong evidence of broken junior pipeline + portfolio bottleneck - this IS the wedge for a new product.
P3DSN-005.credibility: high - reputable design publication with primary interviews

P3DSN-006.title: Product Designers Are Not Being Replaced. They Are Being Sorted.
P3DSN-006.author: Muhammad Dani Asyrofi (Bootcamp/Medium)
P3DSN-006.excerpt: 'More than half [of hiring managers] say they are primarily hiring for senior roles, while only around a quarter are filling junior positions.' '73% of hiring managers want candidates proficient in AI tools.' '91% of designers using AI report it improves quality.' Three emerging hiring archetypes per Jenny Wen: senior strategists, code-adjacent builders (designer-engineers), and 'cracked new grads.' Required skills: strategic judgment, AI literacy, systems thinking, AX (Agent Experience) / MX (Machine Experience). 'Your value was never in pushing rectangles. AI just forced everyone to see that.'
P3DSN-006.relevance: Crystallizes the bifurcation thesis and new skill stack - shows what 'AI capability' means in practice.
P3DSN-006.credibility: medium - opinion piece but draws on industry data and named sources

P3DSN-007.title: Beyond The Hype: What AI Can Really Do For Product Design
P3DSN-007.author: Smashing Magazine
P3DSN-007.excerpt: Practitioner reality check. 'AI is only as good as the questions you ask it...It doesn't replace your thinking.' Three justified concerns: (1) idea generation produces cliches without context, (2) can't build complete user flows, (3) 'AI can't match our visual style. It's easier to just do it by hand.' Works well: micro-interactions, data analysis at scale (30,000 survey responses in 7 languages in 2 hours via Gemini), idea generation with RAG context.
P3DSN-007.relevance: Practitioner-level honest assessment - good for calibration vs vendor hype.

P3DSN-008.title: The Future-Proof Designer
P3DSN-008.author: Nielsen Norman Group
P3DSN-008.excerpt: Authoritative UX voice. 'Design is either becoming more strategic or completely irrelevant.' 'If your role as a designer is limited to polishing UIs or producing high-fidelity prototypes, you've likely been boxed into a narrow definition of design.' Skills to emphasize: systems thinking, storytelling, data judgment (A/B testing, behavioral analytics). Notably does not address portfolio/AI capability display - focuses on irreplaceable human judgment.
P3DSN-008.relevance: Industry-leading voice on what designers need to do; reinforces strategic-shift framing.
P3DSN-008.credibility: high - NN/G is the dominant authority in UX commentary

P3DSN-009.title: Is Dribbble Dead? Why Designers Abandoned Dribbble
P3DSN-009.author: Supercharge Design
P3DSN-009.excerpt: March 2025: Dribbble transitioned from advertising-supported community to revenue-sharing marketplace, hiding contact details until payment. Up to 80% of job requests reported as fake/scams. 'A lot of designs now show that blend of slick surface with uncanny polish' from AI-influenced content. Designers moving to Contra and X (Twitter). Gleb Kuznetsov's deletion 'crystallized what many designers were already feeling: that the trust was gone.'
P3DSN-009.relevance: Documents the death of traditional portfolio platforms - creates opening for new product category.
P3DSN-009.credibility: medium-high - industry blog with specific data and named cases

P3DSN-010.title: Upwork's In-Demand Skills 2026: Demand for top AI skills more than doubles
P3DSN-010.author: Upwork via Stocktitan
P3DSN-010.excerpt: AI-related freelance skills on Upwork grew 109% year over year in 2025. AI video generation/editing: +329% YoY. AI integration: +178%. AI data annotation: +154%. 'Graphic designers already experienced in their field who augment their work with generative AI are seeing significant premiums.' Upwork launched AI Services hub with GPT-4 assistant.
P3DSN-010.relevance: Quantifies market demand growth and premium for AI-augmented designers in freelance market.

P3DSN-011.title: 79+ design statistics: Tools, collaboration, and AI in 2026
P3DSN-011.author: Figma
P3DSN-011.excerpt: 85% of designers and developers say AI will be essential to future success. 78% report AI tools meaningfully accelerate work. Only 58% believe AI improves caliber of deliverables. 40% remain hesitant to fully depend on AI outputs. 33% use AI to generate design assets; 22% use it for initial interface drafts. Generative AI design sector projected from $741M to $13.9B over a decade. 56% express hope about design's future with AI. ~1/3 of creative agencies currently incorporate AI for design/branding (Function Point 2025).
P3DSN-011.relevance: Aggregated stats including agency adoption rate (~33%) - useful baseline.

P3DSN-012.title: Hire a Generative AI Designer: Guide (2025)
P3DSN-012.author: Parallel HQ
P3DSN-012.excerpt: AI-native designer hiring criteria: '(1) familiarity with AI-assisted design tools (Figma's AI features, Midjourney, DALL-E); (2) AI as part of workflow not just a tool - creative loop: Idea -> AI generation -> Human judgment -> AI iteration -> Outcome; (3) data-driven, outcome-focused thinking with success metrics; (4) brand alignment + human judgment to mesh machine intelligence with human taste; (5) building/experimentation through prototypes not just slides.' Hiring shifts toward 'how do you use AI' as core interview question.
P3DSN-012.relevance: Concrete spec of what 'AI capability' means in hiring - product feature implications.
P3DSN-012.credibility: medium - vendor blog but specific actionable criteria

P3DSN-013.title: Anti AI Art: The Growing Movement Defending Digital Artists
P3DSN-013.author: AI Image Detector
P3DSN-013.excerpt: Movement started Dec 5, 2022 with Bulgarian illustrator Alexander Nanitchkov's 'No To AI-Generated Images' graphic. Hashtag #notoaiart spread across DeviantArt and ArtStation. Core demands: consent, credit, compensation. DeviantArt's DreamUp launch caused backlash; platform forced to make all artwork opt-out by default. 'Artists' life's work has been used to build commercial tools that threaten to devalue their skills.'
P3DSN-013.relevance: Documents the active resistance/anti-AI faction within design community - critical for tension framing.
P3DSN-013.credibility: medium - advocacy-leaning but accurate on factual timeline

P3DSN-014.title: Illustrators call out journals and news sites for using AI art
P3DSN-014.author: Nature
P3DSN-014.excerpt: Professional illustrators publicly calling out scientific journals, news organizations for replacing commissioned work with AI-generated imagery. Documented displacement of paid illustration work. Concerns about consent, training data, and devaluation of profession.
P3DSN-014.relevance: Hard evidence of illustration commissions being displaced; mainstream-press validation.

P3DSN-015.title: Ai Graphic Design Jobs on Indeed
P3DSN-015.author: Indeed
P3DSN-015.excerpt: 900+ AI Graphics Designer jobs on Indeed; 392 AI Image Graphic Designer jobs. Sample requirements include: 'proficiency AI graphic design,' 'AI art creator/prompt engineer,' UI/UX designers needed to 'train and improve cutting-edge AI models, assess and enhance how AI systems understand, generate, and evaluate design work.' Skills: Adobe Suite + 'staying current with emerging AI design tools.'
P3DSN-015.relevance: Job-market evidence of explicit AI requirements in design JDs; also documents new 'design-for-AI' role category.

P3DSN-016.title: Gen Ai Ux Designer Jobs
P3DSN-016.author: ZipRecruiter
P3DSN-016.excerpt: Salary range $91k-$170k for Gen AI UX Designer roles. Required: 'Experience with latest AI technologies, including RAG, LLMs, and designing user interfaces that maximize UX'; 'Incorporating AI into UX and UI design'; 'Conversation design principles and interaction patterns for AI interfaces.' Familiarity with OpenAI APIs and user testing platforms typically required.
P3DSN-016.relevance: Concrete JD specs for AI-specific design roles; salary premium evidence.

P3DSN-017.title: AI at Work Report 2025: How GenAI is Rewiring the DNA of Jobs
P3DSN-017.author: Indeed Hiring Lab
P3DSN-017.excerpt: More than a quarter (26%) of jobs posted on Indeed in the past year could be 'highly' transformed by GenAI. Employment growth in industries such as marketing consulting, graphic design, office administration has fallen below trend amid reduced labor demand due to AI-related efficiency gains. Image/video/voice generation capability growth has 'increased automation scores for a range of tasks in media- and web-related occupations.'
P3DSN-017.relevance: Authoritative job-market data showing graphic design specifically among industries with below-trend growth attributed to AI.
P3DSN-017.credibility: high - Indeed's research arm with massive job-posting dataset

P3DSN-018.title: What AI Startups Are Really Looking For: AI-Native Designers
P3DSN-018.author: Yan Liu
P3DSN-018.excerpt: AI startups specifically demanding designers who can 'build, not just design.' Expect designers to ship prototypes with Cursor/Lovable/v0. Look for portfolios that show iterations and prompt history, not just final mockups. Design + engineering blurring; founding designer roles often expect coding capability.
P3DSN-018.relevance: Specific to AI-native startup hiring - which is the leading edge of demand for new-format design portfolios.
P3DSN-018.credibility: medium - opinion piece but well-aligned with corroborating evidence

P3DSN-019.title: Choosing your AI prototyping stack: Lovable, v0, Bolt, Replit, Cursor, Magic Patterns compared
P3DSN-019.author: Anna Arteeva
P3DSN-019.excerpt: Practitioner comparison. Designers increasingly producing working prototypes (not just mockups) via Lovable, v0, Bolt, Cursor, Magic Patterns. 'Vibe coding' became Collins Dictionary's Word of the Year November 2025. Designer-engineer line erasing - non-coding designers can now ship interactive prototypes.
P3DSN-019.relevance: Concrete evidence of design-to-code AI tools reshaping deliverable expectations.
P3DSN-019.credibility: medium - practitioner blog, accurate on tool landscape

P3DSN-020.title: UX Designer Skills: What Hiring Managers Actually Look For in 2025
P3DSN-020.author: UXfolio Blog
P3DSN-020.excerpt: Understanding how AI fits into the user journey is now a core UX skill. 'Designers who can use AI tools like Figma's smart features or Adobe's generative design capabilities are saving time and unlocking creativity.' 'Showing that you're confident with AI and aware of its ethical implications will put you in a stronger position with both employers and recruiters.' Portfolio increasingly expected to demonstrate AI competence.
P3DSN-020.relevance: Portfolio-platform vendor confirms AI-capability is now expected portfolio content.
P3DSN-020.credibility: medium - portfolio vendor (bias) but consistent with broader signal

P3DSN-021.title: Is it ethical to put AI designs in my portfolio as a graphic designer?
P3DSN-021.author: Various designers (Quora)
P3DSN-021.excerpt: Active community debate. Consensus emerging: AI work acceptable in portfolios IF (a) clearly disclosed, (b) presented with explanation of design logic and tool usage, (c) shows human creative judgment. 'Including AI images can be a plus with employers who already incorporate AI in their workflows.' Tension: hiding AI use = ethical breach; showing only AI work = can't evaluate designer's own ability.
P3DSN-021.relevance: Documents the 'is this my work or AI's work' tension - core authorship problem for design portfolios.
P3DSN-021.credibility: low-medium - community discussion but representative of practitioner concern

P3DSN-022.title: Design AI Poses a Threat to Human Designers' Jobs
P3DSN-022.author: Robert H. Smith School of Business (U Maryland)
P3DSN-022.excerpt: Academic research documenting measurable displacement of design tasks by AI. Particularly tasks requiring 'pattern application' rather than novel problem framing. Junior/intermediate roles more exposed than senior strategic roles.

P3DSN-023.title: Agency Performance Review 2025: How AI is rewiring the ad agency workflow
P3DSN-023.author: Campaign US
P3DSN-023.excerpt: By mid-2025, 30% of agencies, brands, publishers had integrated AI into workflows (IAB). Of those who haven't: half anticipate doing so next year. Agencies adopting nearly twice as fast as brands. McKinsey: generative AI can reduce development/prototyping cycles by up to 70% when embedded properly. Strategic question shifted from 'how fast can we generate?' to 'is the output commercially safe, brand-consistent, scalable without drift?'
P3DSN-023.relevance: Agency-side adoption baseline with named sources (IAB, McKinsey).

P3DSN-024.title: The Death of Dribbble: Why Designers Abandoned Their Favorite Platform
P3DSN-024.author: Bootcamp / Medium
P3DSN-024.excerpt: Mirrors Supercharge's analysis. Algorithm rewards 'flashy mockups featuring unreadable type, rainbow gradients, or microinteractions that look cool but would never work in actual products' rather than substantive work. Platform decline aligning with rise of AI-generated portfolio bait. Migrations to X, Contra, personal sites.
P3DSN-024.relevance: Second corroborating source on Dribbble's structural decline.
P3DSN-024.credibility: medium - opinion piece, but consistent with primary source
